e_visa.notes: E-visa mandatory for US nationals as of April 6, 2026. Tourist (TR-V) and Business (TR-N) visa categories available. Apply at cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve. Visa delivered via email. Fee reported at USD 180 (unconfirmed from official Venezuelan source — see gaps). Processing approximately 15 days. Visa valid 1 year, multiple entry.

As of April 2026, Venezuela operates a mandatory electronic visa (e-visa) system for all nationalities that require a visa to enter Venezuela. Applications submitted online at cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve. Visas delivered electronically by email. Tourist (TR-V) and Business (TR-N) visa categories available. Processing time approximately 15 days (reduced from approximately 6 weeks). Visa validity 1 year, multiple entry. Fee amount unconfirmed from official Venezuelan sources; aggregators report USD 180.

visa_policy.notes: The official MPPRE list at detalle_turismo/2 specifies 71 nationalities with visa-free tourism entry by air. All 71 nationalities receive a uniform 90-day stay allowance. Nationalities not on the visa-free list must apply for a visa. Since April 2026, Venezuela operates a mandatory electronic visa (e-visa) system via cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve for all visa-required nationalities, with tourist (TR-V) and business (TR-N) categories. The visa-free list does NOT include the USA, Canada, China, India, Kuwait, Serbia, or most African/Middle Eastern/Asian nations except those specifically listed.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of valid diplomatic and service passports of Serbia are exempt from visa requirements to enter, exit, transit, and remain in Venezuela for up to 90 days. Agreement signed in El Salvador on 6 June 2011; published in Venezuela's Gaceta Oficial No. 39.936 on 24 May 2012. Stay beyond 90 days requires prior authorisation. Holders must cross only through international entry points.

Holders of diplomatic, special (official), and service passports of Oman are exempt from visa requirements pursuant to the visa suppression agreement ('acuerdo de supresión de Visas para pasaportes diplomáticos, especiales y de servicios') signed at the 79th UN General Assembly session in New York on 23 September 2024. Stay limit and other conditions not specified in the MPPRE announcement; full treaty text not yet confirmed in Gaceta Oficial as of validation date.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Kyrgyzstan are exempt from visa requirements pursuant to the visa suppression agreement ('acuerdo de supresión de visas para titulares de pasaportes diplomáticos y de servicio') signed at the 79th UN General Assembly session in New York on 27 September 2024 — the first such agreement between Venezuela and Kyrgyzstan. Stay limit and other conditions not specified in the MPPRE announcement; full treaty text not yet confirmed in Gaceta Oficial as of validation date.

cbi.notes: Venezuela does not have a citizenship-by-investment program. No official CBI program found on any Venezuelan government website. Citizenship can only be obtained after 10 years of continuous lawful residency (or 5 years if married to a Venezuelan citizen), as per the standard naturalization path.

programs.name: Visa de Transeúnte Inversionista (TR-I)
programs.notes: Investor transient visa (TR-I) for foreign nationals who can demonstrate a materialized productive investment in Venezuela. Applicant must hold at least 20% ownership in a registered Venezuelan company or equivalent capital contribution. Visa granted for 3 years (multiple entry, no stay limit), extendable up to 2 additional years. After this, can apply for Resident status (5-year validity). Path to citizenship after 10 years continuous residency (5 years if married to Venezuelan national). No minimum investment amount is specified on the official consular pages. Applications must be submitted at a Venezuelan embassy or consulate.

outbound_official.note: no official Venezuela government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

validation_notes: Re-validated June 2026 against live official MPPRE page (mppre.gob.ve/detalle_turismo/2).
CORRECTED: e-visa tourist category code changed from 'T' to 'TR-V' (correct official Venezuelan category designation); business remains 'TR-N'.

CORRECTED: Removed earlier claim that the e-visa was 'no fee as of April 15, 2026'. Multiple 2026 sources report a fee of USD 180; however, no official Venezuelan government page confirmed fee amounts during this or prior research — noted as unconfirmed gap.

CORRECTED: eligible_nationalities in conditional_access entries changed from string 'any' to specific ISO3 arrays (SRB, OMN, KGZ) since each agreement is bilateral between Venezuela and one specific country, not open to all nationalities.
CONFIRMED: Visa-free list remains exactly 71 countries as listed on mppre.gob.ve/detalle_turismo/2 — re-fetched and matched entry-by-entry.
CONFIRMED: Serbia is NOT on the ordinary-passport visa-free list; its diplomatic/service waiver in conditional_access is correct.
CONFIRMED: Kuwait is NOT on the ordinary-passport visa-free list.
CONFIRMED: All 71 visa-free nationalities receive a uniform 90-day stay; no country-specific shorter limits (e.g., 60 days for Brazil) appear on the official MPPRE page.
CONFIRMED: Saint Lucia ('Santa Lucía') is on the visa-free list — was already in record.
CONFIRMED: Oman (OMN) appears both in visa_free (ordinary passport, 90 days) AND in conditional_access (diplomatic/official/service passports) — these are separate and non-conflicting.
No new visa-free countries added to the official MPPRE list since last validation.
gaps: The official MPPRE page (detalle_turismo/2) does not explicitly state the 90-day maximum stay for visa-free visitors; the 90-day figure is derived from uniform immigration practice and embassies.net confirms 90 days, but the SAIME notice at info.saime.gob.ve/noticia/209 was inaccessible (ECONNREFUSED) during this validation.

The e-visa fee is disputed: non-official aggregators and immigration law firms report USD 180; an earlier aggregator reported no fee as of April 15, 2026. No official Venezuelan government page confirmed fee amounts. Applicants should verify at cancilleriadigital.mppre.gob.ve.
The investor visa TR-I has no minimum investment amount specified on any official consular page accessed.
SAIME info.saime.gob.ve returned DNS/connection failures preventing direct verification of stay limits, e-visa types, and other programs from SAIME pages.

No official data found confirming Venezuela's active participation in Mercosur or Andean Community free-movement frameworks — Venezuela was suspended from Mercosur in 2016 and withdrew from the Andean Community in 2006.
No digital nomad, startup, or fast-track skilled worker visa program found on any official Venezuelan government source.
The complete list of nationalities eligible only for e-visa (vs visa-free) has not been enumerated on any official page accessed.
Cuba, Panama, Honduras, Guatemala, Dominican Republic, Haiti, El Salvador are not on the visa-free list; their e-visa eligibility was not confirmed from official sources.
No official Venezuelan government source found confirming entry benefits for holders of third-country visas (US, Schengen, UK, Canada, etc.) or residence permits.

Approximately 47 additional countries cited in Wikipedia as having diplomatic/service/official passport waivers could not be verified from official Venezuelan government sources (Gaceta Oficial) during this validation. Only Serbia (Gaceta Oficial 2012), Oman (MPPRE 2024), and Kyrgyzstan (MPPRE 2024) were confirmed.
The Oman and Kyrgyzstan conditional_access agreements (signed September 2024) may not yet be formally in force — MPPRE announcements confirm signing but not ratification/entry-into-force dates.
Wikipedia notes the Venezuela-Argentina diplomatic passport waiver has been suspended; this could not be verified from an official Venezuelan government source.
